Plus, a bunch of white van drivers battle it out on the test track, and Michael Gambon is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer travels around the islands and mainland coast of the Caribbean Sea, beginning on Hispaniola. He joins the Dominican Republic polices anti-narcotics division, before crossing the border to Haiti to see the notorious tented slums of Cite Soleil, while also discovering a vibrant, colourful and thriving side to the country. He ends this leg of his journey in Puerto Rico, an archipelago that is a territory of the United States, and examines the legacy of bombing and weapons testing on the island of Vieques

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon meets the owner of a traditional wooden house in Barbados who has turned down millions of dollars from luxury property developers, then encounters marijuana growers on the volcanic island of St Vincent hoping that the drug will soon be decriminalised. On the South American mainland, Simon examines the mismanagement of Venezuelas natural resources, before crossing into Colombia, where he spends time with the Kogi - an indigenous people who have maintained their traditional forest lifestyle

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer and Paul Whitehouse embark on more angling trips round the UK, beginning by heading to North Uist in the Outer Hebrides. Completely unlike anywhere they have fished before, this small, remote and beautiful island has an intricate network of tidal pools and sea lochs, making it near enough equal parts land and water and therefore the perfect place to fish for sea trout

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob and Paul accept an invitation to fish in the estate lake of Burghley House, a 16th-century country house near Stamford in Lincolnshire. Built in the 16th century by William Cecil, Elizabeth Is lord high chancellor, Burghley House is home to some of the most majestic landscapes Paul and Bob have ever found themselves in

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer and Paul Whitehouse are in the Lake District to fish at some of the UKs best-loved beauty spots in the form of Derwentwater and Watendlath Tarn. In a change from the norm, this time Bob has chosen where they go fishing in a poignant trip down memory lane as this was where he used to come on camping holidays with his mates, away from parents for the first time

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Everyday Gourmets
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers demonstrate how to make gourmet food without breaking the bank. They begin with a few weekend treats, rustling up several delicious dishes, gleaning money-saving secrets at the UKs only pub with two Michelin stars and finding out how beer can be as good a match with food as wine. Everything they learn will be put to the test when they help a wounded soldier say thank you to some very important people with a surprise barbecue

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Everyday Gourmets
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers demonstrate how to create a dinner for two that is fit for a king, but doesnt cost the earth. They receive presentation tips from Michelin-starred chef Olivier Limousin and delve into Britains rich history for cut-price culinary inspiration. Everything they learn is put to the test when they help single mother Paula say a big thank-you to her supportive parents

programme.title: Worlds Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Celebrities undertake some of the most perilous journeys in the world. Driving up Madagascars eastern coast at the tail end of the rainy season is enough to test the best of friendships, but comedy actor Angus Deayton and broadcaster Mariella Frostrup are game enough to take on the challenge, and good enough friends to poke fun at each other along the way. The route has numerous river crossings and the duo face a variety of broken bridges and flimsy ferries as the only way across, while the roads themselves are mired with thick mud. Progress becomes increasingly painful and they are finally forced to spend a night in the car

programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal

programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programme.desc: Arson leads to a gunfight as Merseyside Police respond to a pair of attacks on the Wirral that lead to a gang member being hunted

programme.title: Aussie Road Train Truckers
programme.desc: Jack and Hollywood work to deliver $500,000-worth of cattle, Dave is booked to collect a $75,000 ute, and trucker Jorden has broken down and desperately needs help

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: At Golitha Falls, where the River Fowey tumbles down to the sea, Rick meets Dawn French, who has made Cornwall her home. In the shadow of Devonport Dockyard, he boards the worlds busiest chain ferry to cross the wide River Tamar, the ancient watery boundary between Devon and Cornwall - along the way discovering an unusual event involving circus elephant. And he meets a man who thinks he has discovered the next superfood, which Rick used to cook up a delicious duck dish

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick heads into the countys china clay pits to discover what is known locally as white gold, one of its most important industries. He then climbs the Cornish Alps, huge slag heaps created by the clay mining and some of Cornwalls highest land. After a wild swim at one of Cornwalls picturesque hidden coves, Rick cooks a simple sea bass recipe, revealing how to make the perfect mayonnaise. In the far west of the county, on the Lands End peninsula, Rick meets his niece, a modern artist, who explains her deep spiritual connection to Cornwall

programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programme.desc: Documentary series following the investigations of unique specialist units within UK police forces created to crack tough cases ranging from cyber heists, romance fraud and people trafficking to drug rings and murder. The night before Valentine?s Day, 2022, Thames Valley Police receive a 999 call reporting that someone has been attacked close to Banbury town centre - officers are dispatched and find that the victim is 40-year-old Keith Green, who is pronounced dead at the scene and an armed arrest unit conducts an immediate raid on two potentially dangerous suspects. With a possible murder on their doorstep, Thames Valley Major Crime Unit launch Operation Inuit and begin an intense investigation that unravels a love triangle and a killing motivated by twisted passion and male pride.

programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programme.desc: A gunfight in suburban Kent leaves a victim seriously wounded and starts a frenetic high-pressure race to hunt down the perpetrators

programme.title: The Boat That Guy Built
programme.desc: Motorcycle racer Guy Martin renovates a neglected narrowboat using inventions of the Industrial Revolution, beginning with building a blast furnace to cast an iron pot to boil water. He then visits the Wedgwood factory to learn how to create traditional mugs so he can enjoy a cup of tea

programme.title: The Boat That Guy Built
programme.desc: Motorcycle racer Guy Martin continues his renovation quest and makes a steam engine to try to get his narrowboats shower working. He also visits the factory of lavatory manufacturer Thomas Crapper, where he tests 19th-century toilet-flushing systems

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer travels around the islands and mainland coast of the Caribbean Sea, beginning on Hispaniola. He joins the Dominican Republic polices anti-narcotics division, before crossing the border to Haiti to see the notorious tented slums of Cite Soleil, while also discovering a vibrant, colourful and thriving side to the country. He ends this leg of his journey in Puerto Rico, an archipelago that is a territory of the United States, and examines the legacy of bombing and weapons testing on the island of Vieques

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon meets the owner of a traditional wooden house in Barbados who has turned down millions of dollars from luxury property developers, then encounters marijuana growers on the volcanic island of St Vincent hoping that the drug will soon be decriminalised. On the South American mainland, Simon examines the mismanagement of Venezuelas natural resources, before crossing into Colombia, where he spends time with the Kogi - an indigenous people who have maintained their traditional forest lifestyle

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer and Paul Whitehouse embark on more angling trips round the UK, beginning by heading to North Uist in the Outer Hebrides. Completely unlike anywhere they have fished before, this small, remote and beautiful island has an intricate network of tidal pools and sea lochs, making it near enough equal parts land and water and therefore the perfect place to fish for sea trout

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Everyday Gourmets
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers demonstrate how to make gourmet food without breaking the bank. programme.title: Worlds Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Celebrities undertake some of the most perilous journeys in the world. Driving up Madagascars eastern coast at the tail end of the rainy season is enough to test the best of friendships, but comedy actor Angus Deayton and broadcaster Mariella Frostrup are game enough to take on the challenge, and good enough friends to poke fun at each other along the way. The route has numerous river crossings and the duo face a variety of broken bridges and flimsy ferries as the only way across, while the roads themselves are mired with thick mud. Progress becomes increasingly painful and they are finally forced to spend a night in the car

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: At Golitha Falls, where the River Fowey tumbles down to the sea, Rick meets Dawn French, who has made Cornwall her home. In the shadow of Devonport Dockyard, he boards the worlds busiest chain ferry to cross the wide River Tamar, the ancient watery boundary between Devon and Cornwall - along the way discovering an unusual event involving circus elephant. And he meets a man who thinks he has discovered the next superfood, which Rick used to cook up a delicious duck dish

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick heads into the countys china clay pits to discover what is known locally as white gold, one of its most important industries. He then climbs the Cornish Alps, huge slag heaps created by the clay mining and some of Cornwalls highest land. After a wild swim at one of Cornwalls picturesque hidden coves, Rick cooks a simple sea bass recipe, revealing how to make the perfect mayonnaise. In the far west of the county, on the Lands End peninsula, Rick meets his niece, a modern artist, who explains her deep spiritual connection to Cornwall

programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programme.desc: Documentary series following the investigations of unique specialist units within UK police forces created to crack tough cases ranging from cyber heists, romance fraud and people trafficking to drug rings and murder. The night before Valentine?s Day, 2022, Thames Valley Police receive a 999 call reporting that someone has been attacked close to Banbury town centre - officers are dispatched and find that the victim is 40-year-old Keith Green, who is pronounced dead at the scene and an armed arrest unit conducts an immediate raid on two potentially dangerous suspects. With a possible murder on their doorstep, Thames Valley Major Crime Unit launch Operation Inuit and begin an intense investigation that unravels a love triangle and a killing motivated by twisted passion and male pride.

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales venture into the open sea as they make their way through the islands of Scotlands coast. Their destination is the rarely used nine-mile Crinan canal on the Mull of Kintyre, described as the worlds most beautiful shortcut. programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at vibrantly coloured monkeys in Venezuela, Costa Rica and West Africa. He encounters the red howler, which boasts one of the loudest calls in the wild, a capuchin that uses a tree as an anvil, the guenon, which communicates via facial expression, and the ground-dwelling gelada baboon

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the startling similarities between humans and great apes, meeting an orang-utan able to row boats and wash clothes and a gorilla that can communicate using sign language. He also traces the history of human settlement and considers the future of population control and food production

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates this series exploring extreme behaviour in the natural world, uncovering some of the strategies animals have developed to stay alive and to breed, from Komodo dragons tackling buffalo to the courtship battle of the humpback whale. He begins by focusing on the methods used by creatures such as cheetahs, bottle-nosed dolphins and Venus fly-traps in their fights for survival

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how reptiles and amphibians, including the pebble toad and Jesus Christ lizard, conquer their shortcomings by using tricks and strategies to avoid predators. He also explores how chameleons can achieve a high level of accuracy with their muscle-propelled, extendible tongues, and finds out how a 9ft-long Komodo dragon can bring down a huge water buffalo

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: In the second programme, the historian explores how science and detection influenced the popular culture of murder during Victorian times. Writers including Charles Dickens and Wilkie Collins were fascinated by grisly crimes, and the literary genre that came out of it captured the imagination of readers. The presenter also reveals that when Jack the Ripper began his reign of terror in London at the same time Robert Louis Stevensons Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde was published, the idea of the serial killer was introduced to the British public

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville buys too much stock and needs to come up with a way of getting rid of it all. Luckily, the visiting salesman gives him a cunning marketing idea. Meanwhile, Eric is not getting on with his wife, so he seeks emergency romantic advice from Leroy. Can the young Romeo help his hapless friend woo his missus again?

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville struggles to find some privacy when the milkwoman makes a pass at him, so nurse Gladys Emmanuel steps in by persuading Arkwright to take her to a hotel. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason Granville First broadcast in March 1981

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.sub-title: Fig Biscuits and Inspirational Toilet Rolls
programme.desc: Arkwright decides to open the shop before dawn to catch the morning rush and tries a special promotion to get rid of some of the goods, including fig biscuits and toilet rolls

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del takes up cards in his latest bid to make his first million - much to the annoyance of Rodney and Grandad, who reckon he will end up gambling away all their worldly goods. Comedy with the wheeler-dealing Trotter family, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del-Boy cannot believe his ears when Rodney tells him he has found a girlfriend. The trouble is, the woman is old enough to be his mother - and to make matters worse, her husband is about to be released from prison

programme.title: Dads Army
programme.desc: An invasion alert throws Mainwarings hopeless would-be heroes into a panic, leading half the platoon to unwittingly launch an attack on the other half - who are holed up in Godfreys cottage. Comedy from 1969, starring Arthur Lowe and Arnold Ridley

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville buys too much stock and needs to come up with a way of getting rid of it all. Luckily, the visiting salesman gives him a cunning marketing idea. Meanwhile, Eric is not getting on with his wife, so he seeks emergency romantic advice from Leroy. Can the young Romeo help his hapless friend woo his missus again?

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville struggles to find some privacy when the milkwoman makes a pass at him, so nurse Gladys Emmanuel steps in by persuading Arkwright to take her to a hotel. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason Granville First broadcast in March 1981

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: With exciting news concerning the shows new presenting line-up, Alan and Jennie get their teeth into more of the pressing issues of the day. Topics covered on the programme included aggressive Scottishness and vegetarianism and there is a harrowing look at the abolition of corporal punishment. Steve Coogan stars as the broadcaster, with Susannah Fielding, Jonathan Watson and Geoff McGivern

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Now more established as the shows co-host, Alan is able to produce the kind of brave, no-nonsense TV other broadcasters can only dream of. In this edition, he talks police dog bravery and product placement, before meeting his own celebrity lookalike. Steve Coogan stars, with Susannah Fielding

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville tries to stop Madge from moving away and taking Mavis with her - but there is only one person who can change their minds, and he has been dead for years. He also finds himself with a consignment of incredibly sour crab-apple jelly to get rid of. Mrs Featherstone makes Mr Newbold an offer he cannot refuse, and Eric and Cyril vow to be more careful with their money

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Tom sets out to catch the thief responsible for stealing leeks from his garden, but ends up landing himself in trouble. Self-sufficiency comedy, starring Richard Briers, Felicity Kendal and Penelope Keith

programme.title: The Good Life

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary decides to make an honest woman of Dorothy in an effort to prove his commitment, but first has to face the traditional celebration of bachelorhoods end - his stag night. Comedy, starring Martin Clunes, Neil Morrissey, Caroline Quentin and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary finally prepares to give up his freedom but fears he has not slept with enough women, while Dorothy worries that gherkins in the pub will be the only highlight of her special day. Meanwhile, best man Tony has an ulterior motive for making sure the unhappy couple get through their ordeal safely. Comedy, starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Quentin, Neil Morrissey and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Alma treats best friend Leanne to a shopping spree, during which she discovers Bolton Acting School and decides to join up. She also arranges another family meeting between Lin and Joan at an upmarket restaurant, but its not to everybodys taste. Comedy, starring Sophie Willan

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Theres some good news for Lin as the hospital staff inform her that her section could finally be lifted, although this is dependent on her either moving into a hostel or in with a family member. As Alma is finally getting her life back on track and the hostel isnt exactly the right place for a recovering addict, Alma has to convince her grandma to let Lin move in with her - but will this be a step too far for Lin and Joan?

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie uncovers disturbing information regarding his ancestral line. Tylers surprising return from university adds to his sense of worry, convincing him something serious is about to happen. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The staff of Grace Brothers try not to step on one anothers toes when they enter an inter-store ballroom dancing competition. Shop-floor comedy, starring John Inman and Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: In Which Romance Springs a Leak
programme.desc: When Hobbo learns that Howard feels he is taken for granted by Pearl and that Toby misses his former wife, he hatches a plan to sort out their love lives - with the help of flowers, a Mario Lanza recording and his own team of miming violinists

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Variations on a Theme of Fathers Day
programme.desc: Convinced that Nelly is his mother, Hobbo sends Howard to eavesdrop on her and Pearl as he tries to find out more about his father. However, when the women concoct some false information, the police start to take an interest in the mens activities. Comedy, starring Russ Abbot, June Whitfield and Robert Fyfe

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth attends an exclusive auction, while Daisy and Rose try to cool the ardour of a woman who seems determined to marry Daddy. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Judy Cornwell and Josephine Tewson

programme.title: Dads Army: The Lost Episodes
programme.sub-title: The Loneliness of the Long Distance Walker
programme.desc: Remake of the classic sitcom, featuring scripts associated with missing episodes performed by a new cast. Private Walker, source of the platoons supplies of whisky, cigarettes and other essential black-market goods, is called up to serve in the regular Army, so Mainwaring decides to use all his standing as an officer and a bank manager to have him excused from military service. Meanwhile, Jones and the others try to ensure that Walker fails his medical. Kevin McNally stars as Captain Mainwaring, with Robert Bathurst as Sergeant Wilson, Kevin Eldon as Corporal Jones and Mathew Horne as Private Walker

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville faces being left alone at Christmas as all his friends and neighbours make plans to go away. He is particularly dismayed to see Mavis planning a Christmas break with Madge, and terrified to learn that Mr Newbold is plotting an escape from Mrs Featherstones advances, and leaving Granville entirely at her mercy. To make matters worse, the wily shopkeeper has a consignment of dodgy Christmas crackers to sell and a distinct shortage of customers. David Jason stars in the special festive episode

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville tries to stop Madge from moving away and taking Mavis with her - but there is only one person who can change their minds, and he has been dead for years. He also finds himself with a consignment of incredibly sour crab-apple jelly to get rid of. Mrs Featherstone makes Mr Newbold an offer he cannot refuse, and Eric and Cyril vow to be more careful with their money

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: As Lee Mack brings the popular sitcom to an end, a chance to see some of the unintentionally comic moments during filming that ended up on the cutting-room floor

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Guy showers Lucy with expensive gifts before taking her on a mystery weekend away, causing Lee to become suspicious of his motives. He concludes Guy must be a gangster, but Barbara and Tim believe his paranoia about the subject stems from his own yearning for Lucys affections

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville faces being left alone at Christmas as all his friends and neighbours make plans to go away. He is particularly dismayed to see Mavis planning a Christmas break with Madge, and terrified to learn that Mr Newbold is plotting an escape from Mrs Featherstones advances, and leaving Granville entirely at her mercy. To make matters worse, the wily shopkeeper has a consignment of dodgy Christmas crackers to sell and a distinct shortage of customers. David Jason stars in the special festive episode

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville changes his image with the help of some trendy clothes and a medallion made from a cocoa tin lid, while Arkwright decides to increase his profits through advertising

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Tony develops a sudden interest in astrology when Deborah announces her fascination with the mystical art. Meanwhile, Gary disapproves of the new pub landlords policies about lock-ins and bar tabs. Neil Morrissey, Leslie Ash and Martin Clunes star

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Guy showers Lucy with expensive gifts before taking her on a mystery weekend away, causing Lee to become suspicious of his motives. He concludes Guy must be a gangster, but Barbara and Tim believe his paranoia about the subject stems from his own yearning for Lucys affections

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Martin is horrified when Ann decides she needs to broaden her horizons by getting a job. Comedy, starring Richard Briers, Penelope Wilton and Peter Egan

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Now established as co-host of BBC One magazine show This Time, Alan is relishing his career Indian summer and the chance to showcase his broadcasting chops to the nation every weekday following an unfortunate era of working in hyper-local radio and presenting corporate videos. A new make-up artist puts a spring in his step, while a blast from his past returns to make amends. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: When Alma discovers that her care-leaving background can help secure her an audition to join a theatre company for a national tour, she jumps at the chance. However, when social services sends through her records, Alma comes face to face with the stark reality of her childhood growing up in and out of the care system. Could this bombshell lead to Alma missing out on an opportunity shes been waiting her whole life for? Comedy, written by and starring Sophie Willan

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Things are not going well between Lin and Joan and the news that Alma will be leaving for a six-month tour looks like it might push Lin over the edge. When the social worker visits, Lin snaps and disappears, leaving Alma fearing that her mum may have turned back to her old ways

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: The staff treat April to a trip to the cinema - but a mix-up with the projectionist spells disaster for the outing, as an old war film is shown instead of the intended movie. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane, David Griffin and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Dads Army: The Lost Episodes
programme.sub-title: The Loneliness of the Long Distance Walker
programme.desc: Remake of the classic sitcom, featuring scripts associated with missing episodes performed by a new cast. Private Walker, source of the platoons supplies of whisky, cigarettes and other essential black-market goods, is called up to serve in the regular Army, so Mainwaring decides to use all his standing as an officer and a bank manager to have him excused from military service. Meanwhile, Jones and the others try to ensure that Walker fails his medical. Kevin McNally stars as Captain Mainwaring, with Robert Bathurst as Sergeant Wilson, Kevin Eldon as Corporal Jones and Mathew Horne as Private Walker

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville faces being left alone at Christmas as all his friends and neighbours make plans to go away. He is particularly dismayed to see Mavis planning a Christmas break with Madge, and terrified to learn that Mr Newbold is plotting an escape from Mrs Featherstones advances, and leaving Granville entirely at her mercy. To make matters worse, the wily shopkeeper has a consignment of dodgy Christmas crackers to sell and a distinct shortage of customers. David Jason stars in the special festive episode

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville tries to stop Madge from moving away and taking Mavis with her - but there is only one person who can change their minds, and he has been dead for years. He also finds himself with a consignment of incredibly sour crab-apple jelly to get rid of. Mrs Featherstone makes Mr Newbold an offer he cannot refuse, and Eric and Cyril vow to be more careful with their money

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: As Lee Mack brings the popular sitcom to an end, a chance to see some of the unintentionally comic moments during filming that ended up on the cutting-room floor

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Guy showers Lucy with expensive gifts before taking her on a mystery weekend away, causing Lee to become suspicious of his motives. He concludes Guy must be a gangster, but Barbara and Tim believe his paranoia about the subject stems from his own yearning for Lucys affections

programme.desc: A road-rage incident leaves Gary looking a coward - so he decides to save his reputation with a scheme that naturally smacks of disaster. programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee has trouble bonding with his new lodger - a surly 14-year-old boy - but Tim has no such problem and discovers they have a lot in common. Meanwhile, Kate tries to teach a carnivorous dog about the joys of eating vegetables. Sitcom, starring Lee Mack, Megan Dodds, Tim Vine and Rupert Simonian

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Martin is horrified when Ann decides she needs to broaden her horizons by getting a job. Comedy, starring Richard Briers, Penelope Wilton and Peter Egan

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Now established as co-host of BBC One magazine show This Time, Alan is relishing his career Indian summer and the chance to showcase his broadcasting chops to the nation every weekday following an unfortunate era of working in hyper-local radio and presenting corporate videos. A new make-up artist puts a spring in his step, while a blast from his past returns to make amends. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: When Alma discovers that her care-leaving background can help secure her an audition to join a theatre company for a national tour, she jumps at the chance. However, when social services sends through her records, Alma comes face to face with the stark reality of her childhood growing up in and out of the care system. Could this bombshell lead to Alma missing out on an opportunity shes been waiting her whole life for? Comedy, written by and starring Sophie Willan

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Things are not going well between Lin and Joan and the news that Alma will be leaving for a six-month tour looks like it might push Lin over the edge. When the social worker visits, Lin snaps and disappears, leaving Alma fearing that her mum may have turned back to her old ways

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: The staff treat April to a trip to the cinema - but a mix-up with the projectionist spells disaster for the outing, as an old war film is shown instead of the intended movie. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane, David Griffin and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel diagnoses the joint condition osteochondritis dissecans in a hyperactive ridgeback named Loki, and discusses treatment options with his owners. Newfoundland Hugo is referred for his hip dysplasia, but Noel discovers issues with his elbow and knee joints, while Carol and Nigel bring their mischievous seven-month-old Havana kitten Zahra following a mysterious accident at home, which resulted in a broken leg

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: An energetic one-year-old bull terrier puppy called Apple comes into the surgery with an ankle problem, and Noel hopes a custom-made, 3D-printed implant may give this beloved pet a new lease of life. He also treats a two-year-old Siamese cat with a broken leg, shattered pelvis and suspected spinal injury after being hit by a van, while four-year-old collie cross Rosco is tested for hip dysplasia

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A six-month-old collie appears to have a developmental deformity where the bones in its elbows dont fit together, but after CT scans, Noel sees that the situation is even more complex. A Burmese mountain dog has a painful bulging disc, which Noel hopes to replace with a bespoke titanium spacer screw, while cat Elmo has a complex fracture to its left back leg after being hit by a car

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A French bulldog is brought in after collapsing and struggling to move, and after a full medical assessment, Noel realises an exploded disc in the dogs neck is pressuring its spine to such an extent it is slowly causing paralysis. The vet also treats an excitable Staffordshire bull terrier suffering from a problem where the bones in its elbows do not fit properly and a cat that has been shot with an airgun

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats 15-stone English mastiff Bear, who has a ruptured ligament in his knee - but as the dog goes into theatre, a major team effort is needed to get this massive patient through surgery. Siamese cat Bluebell is rushed in after being hit by a car and golden retriever Henry is suffering from a chronically damaged spinal disc

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics treat a pregnant woman who has fallen on her baby bump. Plus, paramedics are called to an unconscious child with a complex medical condition

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics have their hands full with a high-speed motorcycle crash, a woman with abdominal pain and an old lady who has fallen, injuring her face

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Three-year-old Isaac needs an operation on his tongue, a woman has her toes straightened and part of a toe amputated, and a man has biopsies taken from his prostate

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Nigel has keyhole surgery to repair his hernia - but things go wrong when hes back on the ward. Carol has a pin removed from her ankle, which was previously fractured

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Award-winning sketch show, featuring the comedienne as an assortment of quirky and oddball characters, with a guest appearance by Siobhan Redmond

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Ex-EastEnders star Jill Halfpenny and sitcom actor Brian Murphy guest star in the sketch show, featuring Catherine Tate as an assortment of bizarre characters

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: An outspoken school teacher who is very involved in the Latinx community swaps lives with a conservative wife whose husband is a Trump supporter

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: An animal conservationist and professional snake wrangler swaps places with a sport-hunting mother of six, whose family owns a hunting preserve

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: After 15 years of fostering, Stephen and Lynn Smedley were looking forward to their well-earned retirement in Carbis Bay, Cornwall. However, the dream dramatically changed when their daughter died, leaving them caring for their three grandsons. The boys, still grieving for their mums untimely death, are forced to share a single bedroom with a triple bunkbed. There is only one bathroom and Lynn is forced to do the ironing in the shed due to lack of space. Enter Nick Knowles and the team. Their plan is to reconfigure and enlarge their home to accommodate three growing boys - with the help of hundreds of volunteers

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Bangor in Co Down to help Mandy McCreight, who is isolated in her bedroom due to a variety of medical issues and is missing out on family life. Her children Ben and Kara also have mobility problems, while husband Davey looks after everyone, but now he is suffering from a back injury. The entire family are under real stress and the house is not wheelchair friendly, making the situation unbearable

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Barnstaple in Devon to help Jan and Lucy, whose daily four-hour round trips to hospital for life-saving dialysis treatment for two of their daughters is taking a heavy toll on family life. Along with designer Laurence Llewelyn Bowen and hundreds of local volunteers, Nick and the crew extend their house to create a home dialysis centre, enabling the girls to be treated where they live and letting the whole family settle into a healthier and happier routine

programme.title: The Supervet

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Staffordshire Bull Terrier puppy called Rex receives treatment for a badly deformed elbow, and the team carry out a complicated procedure to realign the bones. programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A woman who suffers from nocturnal epileptic fits brings in Pearl, a Staffordshire bull terrier that can sense when these episodes are about to happen. However, the dog has problems with its knees and struggles to walk, so the pressure is on for Noel Fitzpatrick to restore it back to full health. Plus, an Irish setter has a cancerous tumour in its front leg, and a cat needs its jaw wiring back together after being hit by a car

programme.title: Missing Persons Investigation
programme.desc: When police hit a dead end, a young woman stops at nothing to find her missing brother. New breakthrough forensic techniques may hold the secret to a 30-year-old mystery

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Bigger Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz Ashmawy and his team head to Coolaney in Country Sligo to adapt the home of Claire Carpenter to her needs so she can return from hospital after suffering a stroke

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Louis spends time with the global pop star Rita Ora. Louis meets Rita at her dads pub in West London where they sit down for a revealing conversation that takes in her rise to stardom, her familys arrival in the UK in 1990, and the difficulties she has faced dealing with intense press scrutiny and speculation about her personal life. Later, Louis spends time with Rita on the set of her latest music video in LA, before joining her as she puts on a spectacular live performance in Albania

Newfoundland Hugo is referred for his hip dysplasia, but Noel discovers issues with his elbow and knee joints, while Carol and Nigel bring their mischievous seven-month-old Havana kitten Zahra following a mysterious accident at home, which resulted in a broken leg

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: An energetic one-year-old bull terrier puppy called Apple comes into the surgery with an ankle problem, and Noel hopes a custom-made, 3D-printed implant may give this beloved pet a new lease of life. He also treats a two-year-old Siamese cat with a broken leg, shattered pelvis and suspected spinal injury after being hit by a van, while four-year-old collie cross Rosco is tested for hip dysplasia

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A six-month-old collie appears to have a developmental deformity where the bones in its elbows dont fit together, but after CT scans, Noel sees that the situation is even more complex. A Burmese mountain dog has a painful bulging disc, which Noel hopes to replace with a bespoke titanium spacer screw, while cat Elmo has a complex fracture to its left back leg after being hit by a car

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A French bulldog is brought in after collapsing and struggling to move, and after a full medical assessment, Noel realises an exploded disc in the dogs neck is pressuring its spine to such an extent it is slowly causing paralysis. The vet also treats an excitable Staffordshire bull terrier suffering from a problem where the bones in its elbows do not fit properly and a cat that has been shot with an airgun

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats 15-stone English mastiff Bear, who has a ruptured ligament in his knee - but as the dog goes into theatre, a major team effort is needed to get this massive patient through surgery. Siamese cat Bluebell is rushed in after being hit by a car and golden retriever Henry is suffering from a chronically damaged spinal disc

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics treat a pregnant woman who has fallen on her baby bump. Plus, paramedics are called to an unconscious child with a complex medical condition

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics have their hands full with a high-speed motorcycle crash, a woman with abdominal pain and an old lady who has fallen, injuring her face

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Three-year-old Isaac needs an operation on his tongue, a woman has her toes straightened and part of a toe amputated, and a man has biopsies taken from his prostate

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Nigel has keyhole surgery to repair his hernia - but things go wrong when hes back on the ward. Carol has a pin removed from her ankle, which was previously fractured

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Award-winning sketch show, featuring the comedienne as an assortment of quirky and oddball characters, with a guest appearance by Siobhan Redmond

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Ex-EastEnders star Jill Halfpenny and sitcom actor Brian Murphy guest star in the sketch show, featuring Catherine Tate as an assortment of bizarre characters

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: The comediennes bizarre characters run riot as mouthy schoolgirl Lauren visits the gym, wig-makers to the stars Ivan and Trudy go about their business and DI Angie Barker demonstrates how hard it is to be a tough cop and a mother. Una Stubbs guest stars

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Bigger Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz Ashmawy and his team head to Coolaney in Country Sligo to adapt the home of Claire Carpenter to her needs so she can return from hospital after suffering a stroke

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Bigger Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz Ashmawy meets the Mulvey-Sydneys in Ennis in County Clare, where he and his team adapt their home to suit the needs of 13-year-old Isabella, who has a rare congenital disorder

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Canadian comedian, actor and writer, Katherine Ryan, for a frank and honest conversation about her career, relationships and the changing shape of her family. Louis accompanies Katherine as she performs her latest stand-up tour, Missus, in Southend-On-Sea, and later, he spends a rare day off with Katherine, her daughter Violet and her husband Bobby

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets Staffy Boxer Cross Crumble, whos been at the centre in Basildon for over three years, stuck without a foster or permanent home in sight

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: After 15 years of fostering, Stephen and Lynn Smedley were looking forward to their well-earned retirement in Carbis Bay, Cornwall. However, the dream dramatically changed when their daughter died, leaving them caring for their three grandsons. The boys, still grieving for their mums untimely death, are forced to share a single bedroom with a triple bunkbed. There is only one bathroom and Lynn is forced to do the ironing in the shed due to lack of space. Enter Nick Knowles and the team. Their plan is to reconfigure and enlarge their home to accommodate three growing boys - with the help of hundreds of volunteers

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Bangor in Co Down to help Mandy McCreight, who is isolated in her bedroom due to a variety of medical issues and is missing out on family life. Her children Ben and Kara also have mobility problems, while husband Davey looks after everyone, but now he is suffering from a back injury. The entire family are under real stress and the house is not wheelchair friendly, making the situation unbearable

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Barnstaple in Devon to help Jan and Lucy, whose daily four-hour round trips to hospital for life-saving dialysis treatment for two of their daughters is taking a heavy toll on family life. Along with designer Laurence Llewelyn Bowen and hundreds of local volunteers, Nick and the crew extend their house to create a home dialysis centre, enabling the girls to be treated where they live and letting the whole family settle into a healthier and happier routine

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: The odds are stacked against Noel Fitzpatrick and his team as a golden retriever puppy with horrific facial injuries is brought into the practice after being attacked by another dog. Plus, a man drives all the way from Switzerland in the hope of a successful treatment for a Chihuahua, and a woman brings in her Hungarian vizsla, which has a bone and cartilage disorder

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Staffordshire Bull Terrier puppy called Rex receives treatment for a badly deformed elbow, and the team carry out a complicated procedure to realign the bones. Meanwhile, a cross-breed called Piggy with spinal problems leaves its family facing a difficult decision, and Noel devises an unorthodox cure for a stray cat with a badly fractured leg

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A woman who suffers from nocturnal epileptic fits brings in Pearl, a Staffordshire bull terrier that can sense when these episodes are about to happen. However, the dog has problems with its knees and struggles to walk, so the pressure is on for Noel Fitzpatrick to restore it back to full health. Plus, an Irish setter has a cancerous tumour in its front leg, and a cat needs its jaw wiring back together after being hit by a car

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: An outspoken school teacher who is very involved in the Latinx community swaps lives with a conservative wife whose husband is a Trump supporter

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: An animal conservationist and professional snake wrangler swaps places with a sport-hunting mother of six, whose family owns a hunting preserve

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Janine and Adam assist a woman who is threatening to jump out of a first-floor window, and husband and wife team Ann and Kevin are called out to help Anns sick Dad

programme.title: Deepfake Porn: Could You Be Next?
programme.desc: The rising trend of harmless images being turned into hardcore porn, footage that is often used against the people depicted with devastating consequences. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse, including a leading Florida State Senator, Lauren Book, the UK campaigner Kate Isaacs, and Dina, a woman who was deep-faked by a work colleague

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Louis spends time with the global pop star Rita Ora. Louis meets Rita at her dads pub in West London where they sit down for a revealing conversation that takes in her rise to stardom, her familys arrival in the UK in 1990, and the difficulties she has faced dealing with intense press scrutiny and speculation about her personal life. Later, Louis spends time with Rita on the set of her latest music video in LA, before joining her as she puts on a spectacular live performance in Albania

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics are sent to help a woman whose foot was run over by a bin lorry, and a team is sent to assess a man suffering from an agonising headache that has been going on for days.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks drawn the short straw again as he attempts to shift a couple of 1930s Austin Sevens that have been stuck in the same spot for half a century

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: 100 motorbikes come in for auction from every era of the 20th century, and the team assess a 1965 Vanden Plas Princess with a four litre Rolls Royce engine thats seen better days

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rusty 1970 Fiat 500 goes under the hammer - Derek Mathewson reckons its the kind of vehicle that is so iconic that it has to be saved

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Tucked away in the showroom is a WW2-era Willys Jeep. Its original condition means there might be a bunfight for a military vehicle thats ripe for restoration

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in the glens of Antrim, where he is transfixed by tales of fairies and legends of the sea. At the entrance to Belfast Lough in Whitehead he discovers a museum which tells the story of Irelands railway heritage. He also sees if he has what it takes to join the local rowing club. At the medieval Carrickfergus Castle, Michael learns of the towns important role in the history of Ireland, when William of Orange landed at its harbour and went on to fight the Battle of the Boyne, which is commemorated by Unionists to this day

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Bangor, County Down, en route to the Northern Irish capital of Belfast. Along the way he enjoys live music on a boat, stops off at the imposing parliament building at Stormont and explores the regenerated Titanic Quarter on the Maritime Mile

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his railway journey along the Atlantic and Irish Sea coasts of Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland. This time, he makes his way from the vast beaches of the Carlingford Peninsula towards Drogheda and Newgrange, before arriving in capital of the Republic of Ireland, Dublin, where he visits Dublin Castle and Dublin docks

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from the capital of the Republic of Ireland, Dublin, on the DART suburban railway to the harbour town of Dun Laoghaire. He joins the crew of the RV Malice to survey what lies beneath the waves in Dublin Bay. Back on dry land, the DART takes Michael to his next stop, Sandycove, where James Joyce, one of Irelands greatest writers, found inspiration

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the myth of German invincibility was finally debunked by their defeat at the Battle of Stalingrad. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How German U-boats were used to disrupt the flow of goods from America across the Atlantic Ocean in an attempt to starve Britain into submission

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Account of Operation Barbarossa, Germanys plan to invade the Soviet Union in 1941, revealing the massive human and financial cost endured by the Soviet people, who fought a bloody and lonely war with little support from the Allies. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Bomber Command is unable to hit its targets precisely and attacks German cities under cover of darkness, while the Blitz in Britain brings public demand for revenge strikes, and Arthur Harris takes charge of Bomber Command

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: On the last leg of his epic journey across the north of England, narrowboater Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Ouse and is forced to make an emergency stop. His destination is Ripon Basin at the outer reaches of Britains inland waterways

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a fresh set of adventures along the waterways of the north of England and the Midlands. He is forced to make a running repair to his narrowboat the Naughty Lass while on the Tinsley lock flight in South Yorkshire. He then cruises beneath Conisbroughs magnificent viaduct and must wait for an unusual sliding railway bridge to open on the Stainforth and Keadby Canal

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Trent and is invited to join a night-time illuminated boat parade. However, his journey is hampered by a damaged wall in Worksop and a water shortage on the Chesterfield Canal which threatens his voyage

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Autumnal fog and an overheating engine prove to be problems for narrowboater Robbie Cumming. As he navigates a Roman waterway, it proves to be a journey of many challenges as he runs aground and breaks down in Nottingham

programme.title: Britains Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.desc: Andy Torbet and local seafaring volunteers build a boat made of just willow and cow hide, and set out to cross the dangerous Pentland Firth as the ancient Orcadians would have done. Neil Oliver investigates the extraordinary discovery of some human bones, Chris Packham goes in search of whales and Shini Somara uncovers the powers of the tides

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of two. Simon explores the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examines the rich culture and troubled past of the island. Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. The second leg of Simons journey begins at the Giants Causeway, before visiting a kelp farm on Rathlin Island. He heads to the Dark Hedges in Co Antrim, one of the filming locations from Game of Thrones, and visits Belfast for the July 12 parades, discovering how the event has divided the city. He also learns more about Oliver Cromwells influence on Ireland, attends the Gaze film festival, and meets monkeys at an animal sanctuary in the Wicklow mountains

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming sets out to explore the meandering waterways of inner city Birmingham including the Soho Loop and the Engine Arm Canal

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the worlds caves, including the Lechuguilla Cave in America, which contains chambers filled with 6m-long crystals. He observes creatures in Texas and Thailand that never see daylight, and inside Deer Cave in Borneo he finds five million bats whose droppings support an entire ecosystem

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The nature series looks at animals that live on some of the worlds highest mountains. Cameras show gelada baboons screaming to frighten off predators in Ethiopia, demoiselle cranes struggling to migrate over the Himalayas, snow leopards hunting on the peaks of Pakistan and a wild giant panda nursing a baby in a hillside cave. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob and Terry seek sanctuary from the news so they can watch TV highlights of an England v Bulgaria football match without knowing the result, but they are pursued by the relentless Flint, who has bet them they cannot escape the final score. James Bolam and Rodney Bewes star, with Brian Glover and Donald Gee

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: The ever-optimistic Bob tries to broaden Terrys social circle by inviting him to a trendy dinner party, but as usual his well-meaning efforts result in disaster. Rodney Bewes and James Bolam star as the original men behaving badly

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Thelma looks on with disapproval as Terry helps Bob move his most treasured possessions to his new house, releasing a flood of nostalgic memories about their youthful misadventures. Comedy, starring Rodney Bewes and James Bolam

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks baby Max makes his podium debut with Great Grandad Derek. Paul visits a former stock car racer and finds a Pinto pimped Anglia and a Talbot Sunbeam Lotus

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team head for restoration in their Bedford Rascal Campervan. They also purchase a true British classic - a TR6 - which is almost too good to touch!

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to North Wales to collect an Austin 7, and a gem of a Bentley, a British classic with coachwork built by top notch Parisian outfit Kellner who specialised in luxury

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Doncaster to collect four vintage BSAs motorbikes including one said to be the rarest BSA of all

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek makes the short trip to a mysterious Yorkshire beauty spot to pick up a 1920s style bus that isnt all that it seems

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels across Dorset, beginning in Portland Harbour, home to some of the finest sailing waters in the UK and the location for the 2012 Olympic sailing events. In the county town of Dorchester, Michael hears the story of the Tolpuddle Martyrs, who paved the way for todays trade unions

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo alights at Wareham to visit Dorsets internationally renowned Jurassic Coast. He heads through Swanage, Poole and Sandbanks, before ending his journey on Brownsea Island

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads from a bustling Southampton to the Isle of Wight, where he rides the islands railway, made up of recycled London Underground trains

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos journey has brought him to Lee on the Solent in Hampshire and the training centre for the coastguard. He also visits the great maritime city of Portsmouth, and then travels eastwards to Chichesters vast, natural harbour

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team gamble on restoring a Vanden Plas saloon from the 1970s and a childs tricycle from the 1960s. Plus, catch up on the restoration of a rare Sunbeam Lotus

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek encourages the team to buy a Francis Barnett Plover at auction. Plus, the wheels come off the restoration of an MG Metro

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The longest retreat in British military history, when Allied troops defending Burma in 1942 were confronted by Japanese forces well prepared for jungle warfare. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The story of Hitlers personal secretary Martin Bormann and the efforts to track him down. With insufficient evidence to confirm the Nazi officials death, the International Military Tribunal at Nuremberg tried him in absentia during 1946 and eventually sentenced him to death. Despite extensive efforts, he was never found and was eventually presumed to have died in May 1945

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Glasgows Pollok Park, where finds include an incredibly rare Victoria Cross medal awarded to a Sikh soldier in the Second World War, three modern wooden sculptures discovered in the free bin outside a charity shop, a painting by local artist Edward Atkinson Hornel, and a diamond necklace that was given by the last kaiser of Germany to a British governess

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Swanage Pier on Dorsets Jurassic Coast, where treasures include dinosaur footprints, a pottery pelican, a collection of waterline model boats, a valuable Art Deco bronze sculpture, and original designs for the coronet for King Charles, created for his Investiture as Prince of Wales in 1969

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The Restos team try their hardest not to go too far on an Opel Manta. And a Peugeot 205 GTI brings back happy B-road memories for a mechanic who owned one

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team head for restoration in their Bedford Rascal Campervan. They also purchase a true British classic - a TR6 - which is almost too good to touch!

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The longest retreat in British military history, when Allied troops defending Burma in 1942 were confronted by Japanese forces well prepared for jungle warfare. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The story of Hitlers personal secretary Martin Bormann and the efforts to track him down. With insufficient evidence to confirm the Nazi officials death, the International Military Tribunal at Nuremberg tried him in absentia during 1946 and eventually sentenced him to death. Despite extensive efforts, he was never found and was eventually presumed to have died in May 1945

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough focuses on animals living in deserts. Cameras explore the dry Atacama of Chile, where guanacos survive by licking dew off cacti, and capture the rare sight of wild camels eating snow to stay hydrated on Mongolias Gobi plains. In the rocky Namibian desert, elephants desperately search for food and lions wander the wilderness hunting oryx

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Camera crews follow the activities of wildlife in the Arctic and Antarctic, including an emperor penguin colonys nine-month migration across sea ice to its breeding ground. The team also takes aerial and underwater shots to capture humpback whales feeding and tracks a male polar bear as it swims 100km to find new ice from which to hunt seals

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores New South Wales by train, travelling 570 miles from Sydney to the mining city of Broken Hill. He explores the origins of Sydney as a penal colony, visiting the Hyde Park barracks where many of the first convicts were housed. He also tours the citys iconic opera house, and volunteers for the lifesaving club at Bondi Beach. In the Blue Mountains, he learns about peoples relationship to the land from his indigenous guide, before reaching Katoomba, where he rides the steepest railway in the world

programme.title: Locomotion: Dan Snows History of Railways
programme.desc: The historian and broadcaster charts the development of the UKs rail network, from its beginnings as a primitive system of trackways for coal carts in the early 18th century up to the present day. Rapid industrial growth during the 19th century, coupled with the prospect of vast profits, drove inventors and entrepreneurs to develop steam locomotives, metal tracks and an array of daring tunnels, cuttings and bridges that created a nationwide system of railways in just 30 years. George Stephensons Liverpool and Manchester Railway became the model for future inter-city travel and his fast, reliable locomotive the Rocket began a quest for speed that has defined the modern world

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The Restos team try their hardest not to go too far on an Opel Manta. And a Peugeot 205 GTI brings back happy B-road memories for a mechanic who owned one

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team, including Andy Joynson, buy the right kind of Land Rover, but its got the wrong engine. Can they clean up its emissions with a modern motor?

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: British car enthusiasts Will Trickett and Gus Gregory have just one week to fix neglected cars and double their selling price. Here, the duo head to London to buy a 73 Mk 4 Spitfire. It seems solid, but how it fares on the road remains to be seen

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien travel across the UK to find unique and valuable items, and their latest find is an old bureau which gets a makeover in Yorkshire

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: The historian examines Britains deadliest pandemic, exploring how its monumental death toll changed the face of the country for ever and what the sudden loss of life meant for the church and religious beliefs, landowners and for the those who survived. Lucy also learns how DNA extracted from the skeletons of the plagues victims enabled scientists to finally identify a bacteria called Yersinia Pestis - a pathogen to which the mediaeval population had no immunity

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from the capital of the Republic of Ireland, Dublin, on the DART suburban railway to the harbour town of Dun Laoghaire. He joins the crew of the RV Malice to survey what lies beneath the waves in Dublin Bay. Back on dry land, the DART takes Michael to his next stop, Sandycove, where James Joyce, one of Irelands greatest writers, found inspiration

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the myth of German invincibility was finally debunked by their defeat at the Battle of Stalingrad. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How German U-boats were used to disrupt the flow of goods from America across the Atlantic Ocean in an attempt to starve Britain into submission

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Account of Operation Barbarossa, Germanys plan to invade the Soviet Union in 1941, revealing the massive human and financial cost endured by the Soviet people, who fought a bloody and lonely war with little support from the Allies. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Bomber Command is unable to hit its targets precisely and attacks German cities under cover of darkness, while the Blitz in Britain brings public demand for revenge strikes, and Arthur Harris takes charge of Bomber Command

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: On the last leg of his epic journey across the north of England, narrowboater Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Ouse and is forced to make an emergency stop. His destination is Ripon Basin at the outer reaches of Britains inland waterways

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a fresh set of adventures along the waterways of the north of England and the Midlands. He is forced to make a running repair to his narrowboat the Naughty Lass while on the Tinsley lock flight in South Yorkshire. He then cruises beneath Conisbroughs magnificent viaduct and must wait for an unusual sliding railway bridge to open on the Stainforth and Keadby Canal

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Trent and is invited to join a night-time illuminated boat parade. However, his journey is hampered by a damaged wall in Worksop and a water shortage on the Chesterfield Canal which threatens his voyage

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Autumnal fog and an overheating engine prove to be problems for narrowboater Robbie Cumming. As he navigates a Roman waterway, it proves to be a journey of many challenges as he runs aground and breaks down in Nottingham

programme.title: Britains Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.desc: Andy Torbet and local seafaring volunteers build a boat made of just willow and cow hide, and set out to cross the dangerous Pentland Firth as the ancient Orcadians would have done. Neil Oliver investigates the extraordinary discovery of some human bones, Chris Packham goes in search of whales and Shini Somara uncovers the powers of the tides

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of two. Simon explores the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examines the rich culture and troubled past of the island. Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. The second leg of Simons journey begins at the Giants Causeway, before visiting a kelp farm on Rathlin Island. He heads to the Dark Hedges in Co Antrim, one of the filming locations from Game of Thrones, and visits Belfast for the July 12 parades, discovering how the event has divided the city. He also learns more about Oliver Cromwells influence on Ireland, attends the Gaze film festival, and meets monkeys at an animal sanctuary in the Wicklow mountains

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming sets out to explore the meandering waterways of inner city Birmingham including the Soho Loop and the Engine Arm Canal

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the worlds caves, including the Lechuguilla Cave in America, which contains chambers filled with 6m-long crystals. He observes creatures in Texas and Thailand that never see daylight, and inside Deer Cave in Borneo he finds five million bats whose droppings support an entire ecosystem

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The nature series looks at animals that live on some of the worlds highest mountains. Cameras show gelada baboons screaming to frighten off predators in Ethiopia, demoiselle cranes struggling to migrate over the Himalayas, snow leopards hunting on the peaks of Pakistan and a wild giant panda nursing a baby in a hillside cave. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob and Terry seek sanctuary from the news so they can watch TV highlights of an England v Bulgaria football match without knowing the result, but they are pursued by the relentless Flint, who has bet them they cannot escape the final score. James Bolam and Rodney Bewes star, with Brian Glover and Donald Gee

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: The ever-optimistic Bob tries to broaden Terrys social circle by inviting him to a trendy dinner party, but as usual his well-meaning efforts result in disaster. Rodney Bewes and James Bolam star as the original men behaving badly

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Thelma looks on with disapproval as Terry helps Bob move his most treasured possessions to his new house, releasing a flood of nostalgic memories about their youthful misadventures. Comedy, starring Rodney Bewes and James Bolam

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks baby Max makes his podium debut with Great Grandad Derek. Paul visits a former stock car racer and finds a Pinto pimped Anglia and a Talbot Sunbeam Lotus

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Doncaster to collect four vintage BSAs motorbikes including one said to be the rarest BSA of all

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
